questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Adding $\mathrm{H}^{\oplus}$ to a given anion may significantly change the bond length between existing atoms of the original anion due to change in their bond order. This is observed on addition of $\mathrm{H}^{\oplus}$ to how many of the following anions?
$\mathrm{TeO}_6^{6-}, \mathrm{IO}_6^{5-}, \mathrm{XeO}_6^{4-}, \mathrm{C}_2^{2-}, \mathrm{O}_2^{2-}, \mathrm{N}_3^{--}, \mathrm{I}_3^{-}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
In how many of the following species the central atoms have two lone pairs of electrons?
$\begin{array}{lll}\mathrm{XeF}_4 & \mathrm{XeF}_5^{-} & \mathrm{F}_2 \mathrm{SeO}_2 \\\mathrm{XeF}_3^{+} & \mathrm{XeOF}_4 & \mathrm{ClOF}_3 \\\mathrm{ICl}_4^{-} & \mathrm{SCl}_2 & \mathrm{OSF}_4\end{array}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
In how many of the following molecules/ions, the number of electrons in $\sigma$ molecular orbital (both $\sigma \& \sigma$ ) are more than or equal to 9 . (In ground state)
(a) $\mathrm{N}_2$
(b) $\mathrm{N}_2^{+}$
(c) $\mathrm{Be}_2^{+}$
(d) $\mathrm{B}_2^{-}$
(e) $\mathrm{B}_2$
(f) $\mathrm{C}_2^{2-}$
(g) $\mathrm{C}_2$
(h) $\mathrm{H}_2$
(i) $\mathrm{Li}_2^{+}$
(j) $\mathrm{O}_2^{+}$
(k) $\mathrm{F}_2^{+}$
questions.solution:
$\because \quad$ for molecules upto $\mathrm{N}_2, \sigma 1 \mathrm{~s}^2 \sigma^* 1 \mathrm{~s}^2 \sigma 2 \mathrm{~s}^2 \sigma^* 2 \mathrm{~s}^2 \pi 2 \mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{x}}^2=$ $\pi 2 p_y^2 \sigma 2 p_z^2$ order is followed.
Hence in
(a) $10 \mathrm{e}^{-}$
(b) $9 e^{-}$
(c) $7 \mathrm{e}^{-}$
(d) $8 \mathrm{e}^{-}$
(e) $8 \mathrm{e}^{-}$
(f) $10 \mathrm{e}^{-}$
(g) $8 \mathrm{e}^{-}$
(h) $2 \mathrm{e}^{-}$
(i) $5 \mathrm{e}^{-}$
are present
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The number of equivalent contributing structures possible for $\mathrm{XeO}_6^{4-}$ is m . The bond order of $\mathrm{Xe}-\mathrm{O}$ bond is $n$. Find $(m \times n) \times 0.2$. Round off your answer to the nearest integer.
questions.solution:
Bond order of $\mathrm{XeO}_6^{4-}$ is $4 / 3=n$
It has 15 possible structures $\therefore \quad m=15$ Thus $\mathrm{mn}=15 \times \frac{4}{3}=20 \quad \therefore \quad 0.2 \mathrm{mn}=0.2 \times 20=4$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Among the following species <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msubsup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></msubsup><mo>,</mo><msubsup><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></msubsup><mo>,</mo><msubsup><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>2</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msubsup><mo>,</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msubsup><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></msubsup><mo>,</mo><msubsup><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></msubsup><mo>,</mo><msubsup><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msubsup></math> the number of species showing diamagnetism is
questions.solution:
The molecules with unpaired electrons are paramagnetic and molecules with all electrons paired are diamagnetic. In general molecules with an even number of electrons (except 10 and 16 electrons) are diamagnetic

N214e- diamagnetic

N2+13e- paramagnetic 

N2-15e-paramagnetic

N22-16e- paramagnetic

O216e-paramagnetic

O2+15e-paramagnetic

O2-17e-paramagnetic

O22-18e-diamagnetic
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
The number of molecule(s) or ion(s) from the following having non-planar structure is______.

NO3-,H2O2,BF3,PCl3,XeF4,

SF4,XeO3,PH4+,SO3,AlOH4-
questions.solution:
The species which have sp2 hybridisation with no lone pairs, sp3d2 hybridisation with two lone pairs are planar molecules.

The number of hybrid orbitals can be calculated as follows,

No.of hybrid orbitals = 1/2(V+M-C+A)

V = number of valence electrons

M = number of monovalent atoms attached to central atom.

C = Number of positive charges on the molecule

A = Number of negative charges on the molecule

 

SO3 sp2 Planar
BF3 sp2 Planar
NO3- sp2 Planar
SF4 sp3d Non-Planar
H2O2 sp3 Non-Planar
PCl3 sp3 Non-Planar
AlOH4- sp3 Non-Planar
XeF4 sp3d2 Planar
XeO3 sp3 Non-Planar
PH4+ sp3 Non-Planar
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: How many lone pairs are present on the central atom in $\left[\mathrm{ICl}_4\right]$.
questions.solution:
2 lone pair on I.
$\mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^2$ hybridised with square planar geometry in $\left[\mathrm{ICl}_4^{-}\right]$
